title: Chromatographic analysis of glutamic acid decarboxylase in biological samples.
abstract: A number of non-chromatographic and chromatographic methods for analysis of GAD activity in biological tissues have been described. The majority of these chromatographic methods utilize the analysis of GABA formed from incubation homogenates. Depending upon the analytical technique selected, limits of detection range from nanogram to picogram levels of GABA. Also discussed have been some of the commonly associated problems and their resolution with sample collection, postmortem changes and alternative pathways of CO2 and GABA production which can lead to errors in accurate determination of GAD activity in biological samples.
